rules: [P1] Strictly evaluate conditional triggers and boolean constraints before initiating state-changing actions.
[P1] Validate tool output and state-change persistence (transaction ID) before generating final responses.
[P1] Reject records outside specified temporal windows and document record age in the thought trace.
[P1] Verify existence of patient records and identity via tool call before extracting attributes or performing state-changing operations.
[P1] Prioritize structured key-value extraction over narrative prose.
[P1] Exhaustively verify data absence (query synonyms, broader windows) before concluding a null result.
[P1] Explicitly retrieve and cite tool-provided reference ranges and threshold values; avoid internal knowledge for interpretation.
[P1] Perform explicit date arithmetic (time delta) between retrieved records and current system time; document the evaluation in the thought trace.
[P1] Separate the decision to act from the execution; confirm actions only after tool logs verify completion.
[P1] Treat missing tool output as a blocking error; do not fabricate values or guess when retrieval fails.
[P1] Use a 'plan-and-execute' structure: decompose workflows into distinct phases and checklist sub-tasks.
[P1] Validate clinical payloads against tool schema requirements before execution.
[P1] Mandate tool usage for all data-dependent queries; never substitute internal knowledge for external execution.
[P1] Prioritize data retrieval and conditional assessment in early turns to preserve task budget.
[P1] Map unstructured clinical requests into standardized SBAR frameworks prior to tool submission.
[P1] Standardize clinical entries with ISO 8601 timestamps and dual patient identifiers.
